Best usable claim: Technology based, company specific interview rehearsal with CV plus JD analysis, prep packs, spoken practice and honest readiness feedback.
Weak assumption to test: That users understand and value the full product in plain English, without it sounding like generic AI interview prep.
Proof needed before final submission: Mock product walkthrough with 5 target users. Ask them to repeat back what it does and what they would pay for.

Best usable claim: Founder has demonstrated product discipline, research discipline and ability to build and debug a real AI workflow.
Weak assumption to test: That the founder story is strong enough without a conventional commercial or technical cofounder signal.
Proof needed before final submission: Write a tight founder narrative with evidence of execution, domain insight, customer discovery and resilience.

Best usable claim: Solo founder with clear missing roles identified.
Weak assumption to test: That New Frontiers will accept gaps if the plan to fill them is credible.
Proof needed before final submission: Name at least 2 advisors or active mentor prospects, ideally technical AI, career services or HR, and commercial sales.

Best usable claim: There is evidence of project management through product build, architecture decisions, validation planning and disciplined scope control.
Weak assumption to test: That this can be translated into business language rather than internal build history.
Proof needed before final submission: Convert build history into milestones, risks resolved, decisions made and lessons learned.

Best usable claim: Paid interview prep exists; paid online market is estimated around $0.5B, with IE plus UK early pool estimated around $25M.
Weak assumption to test: IE plus UK SOM is thin and willingness to pay rate is the weakest sizing assumption.
Proof needed before final submission: Refresh sources, verify public numbers, and collect primary evidence from 12 to 15 past payers.

Best usable claim: Segment A: candidates with a scheduled interview at a named company, who need company specific preparation quickly and affordably.
Weak assumption to test: That this segment ranks company specificity above price and pays €49.
Proof needed before final submission: Past payer AJTBD interviews plus real landing page checkout test.

Best usable claim: Generic AI mock tools are crowded; human coaches own company specific prep; the proposed wedge is company specific plus honest scoring plus instant availability.
Weak assumption to test: Competitor data may be stale or secondary, and some claims need direct source verification.
Proof needed before final submission: Updated competitor table with prices, target customers, strengths, weaknesses and citations.

Best usable claim: Ireland is a beachhead; UK gives a more credible early market; English language markets provide expansion logic.
Weak assumption to test: International growth path is not yet validated by channels or CAC.
Proof needed before final submission: Define sequence: Ireland tech hubs, UK tech candidates, then English speaking EU/US niches only after unit economics work.

Best usable claim: No competitor appears to hold all four: company specific, honest calibrated feedback, available immediately, affordable versus a coach.
Weak assumption to test: Honest AI judging is both the differentiator and the trust risk.
Proof needed before final submission: Side by side comparison against ChatGPT and human coach alternative.

Best usable claim: Per cycle pricing around €49, later bundles or credits, low automated COGS if model costs are controlled.
Weak assumption to test: CAC and LTV may not close because usage is episodic.
Proof needed before final submission: Real checkout conversion, CAC test and actual model cost measurement.

Best usable claim: Potential reference customers can be named as prospects, not customers.
Weak assumption to test: Market validation without paying users is weak for Phase 2.
Proof needed before final submission: Secure 5 to 10 interviews, 3 LOIs or pilots, or 10 paid concierge cycles.

Best usable claim: Research completed, segment map, competitor scan, risk register and validation plan; product build exists but voice reliability is unresolved.
Weak assumption to test: Build progress may be less persuasive than customer evidence.
Proof needed before final submission: Present milestones as learning milestones plus validation milestones, not just feature milestones.

Best usable claim: Can describe staged growth from concierge validation to automated consumer product, then B2B2C or UK expansion.
Weak assumption to test: Revenue and hiring targets are not yet grounded.
Proof needed before final submission: Conservative, base and stretch projections tied to conversion, ARPU, CAC and customer volumes.

Best usable claim: This is the strongest area: 6 week validation plan, then build or pivot decision, then product hardening and customer acquisition.
Weak assumption to test: Goals must match New Frontiers two month review rhythm.
Proof needed before final submission: Turn into month 2, month 4 and month 6 milestones with KPIs.
